The POST form at 'http://localhost/vulnerabilities/exec/' (from 'http://localhost/vulnerabilities/exec/') does not contain a CSRF token. Without a token, attackers can trick authenticated users into submitting this form from any website.
vulnerabilities.evidence: POST form fields: ['ip', 'Submit'] — none match known CSRF token names

vulnerabilities.description: The form at 'http://localhost/vulnerabilities/javascript/' has a CSRF token field ('token') but the server accepted a completely fake token value. The token exists in the HTML but is not validated server-side.
vulnerabilities.evidence: Server returned HTTP 200 with fake token — no rejection detected
